sections.heading: TNM
sections.content:
AJCC 8th Edition (2017) TNM Staging System for Small Intestine Adenocarcinoma.

t_stages: [
  {"stage": "TX", "description": "Primary tumor cannot be assessed."},
  {"stage": "T0", "description": "No evidence of primary tumor."},
  {"stage": "Tis", "description": "High-grade dysplasia/carcinoma in situ."},
  {"stage": "T1", "description": "Tumor invades the lamina propria or submucosa."},
  {"stage": "T1a", "description": "Tumor invades the lamina propria."},
  {"stage": "T1b", "description": "Tumor invades the submucosa."},
  {"stage": "T2", "description": "Tumor invades the muscularis propria."},
  {"stage": "T3", "description": "Tumor invades through the muscularis propria into the subserosa, or extends into nonperitonealized perimuscular tissue (mesentery or retroperitoneum) without serosal penetration."},
  {"stage": "T4", "description": "Tumor perforates the visceral peritoneum or directly invades other organs or structures (e.g., other loops of small intestine, mesentery of adjacent loops of bowel, and abdominal wall by way of serosa; for duodenum only, invasion of pancreas or bile duct)."}
]

n_stages: [
  {"stage": "NX", "description": "Regional lymph nodes cannot be assessed."},
  {"stage": "N0", "description": "No regional lymph node metastasis."},
  {"stage": "N1", "description": "Metastasis in one or two regional lymph nodes."},
  {"stage": "N2", "description": "Metastasis in three or more regional lymph nodes."}
]

m_stages: [
  {"stage": "M0", "description": "No distant metastasis."},
  {"stage": "M1", "description": "Distant metastasis present."}
]

stage_groups: [
  {"group": "Stage 0", "criteria": "Tis, N0, M0", "clinical_meaning": "High-grade dysplasia or carcinoma in situ confined to the epithelium.", "five_yr_survival": "Not explicitly stated; excellent prognosis with local treatment.", "treatment_intent": "Curative (endoscopic or surgical resection)."},
  {"group": "Stage I", "criteria": "T1-2, N0, M0", "clinical_meaning": "Tumor invades lamina propria, submucosa, or muscularis propria but not beyond. No lymph node or distant metastasis.", "five_yr_survival": "Localized disease has 5-year relative survival of approximately 85% [Discussion MS-10].", "treatment_intent": "Curative. Primary treatment is surgical resection."},
  {"group": "Stage IIA", "criteria": "T3, N0, M0", "clinical_meaning": "Tumor invades through muscularis propria into subserosa or nonperitonealized perimuscular tissue. No lymph node or distant metastasis.", "five_yr_survival": "Prognosis worse than stage I; survival data not explicitly given.", "treatment_intent": "Curative. Surgery is primary. Adjuvant therapy may be considered based on risk features."},
  {"group": "Stage IIB", "criteria": "T4, N0, M0", "clinical_meaning": "Tumor perforates serosa or invades adjacent organs/structures. No lymph node or distant metastasis.", "five_yr_survival": "Prognosis worse than stage IIA; survival data not explicitly given.", "treatment_intent": "Curative. Surgery is primary. Adjuvant therapy is commonly recommended."},
  {"group": "Stage IIIA", "criteria": "Any T, N1, M0", "clinical_meaning": "Tumor of any depth, with metastasis in 1-2 regional lymph nodes. No distant metastasis.", "five_yr_survival": "Stage III disease has significantly worse outcomes compared to earlier stages [Discussion MS-6].", "treatment_intent": "Curative with multimodal therapy. Adjuvant chemotherapy is recommended."},
  {"group": "Stage IIIB", "criteria": "Any T, N2, M0", "clinical_meaning": "Tumor of any depth, with metastasis in ≥3 regional lymph nodes. No distant metastasis.", "five_yr_survival": "Poor prognosis; survival data not explicitly given.", "treatment_intent": "Curative with multimodal therapy. Adjuvant chemotherapy is recommended."},
  {"group": "Stage IV", "criteria": "Any T, Any N, M1", "clinical_meaning": "Distant metastatic disease present.", "five_yr_survival": "5-year relative survival of only approximately 42% for stage IV disease [Discussion MS-10].", "treatment_intent": "Palliative. Treatment with systemic therapy; curative intent only in highly selected cases with resectable metastases."}
]

t_stages.description: Tumor invades the lamina propria or submucosa.
Tumor invades through the muscularis propria into the subserosa, or extends into nonperitonealized perimuscular tissue (mesentery or retroperitoneum) without serosal penetration.

Tumor perforates the visceral peritoneum or directly invades other organs or structures (e.g., other loops of small intestine, mesentery of adjacent loops of bowel, and abdominal wall by way of serosa; for duodenum only, invasion of pancreas or bile duct).

n_stages.description: Regional lymph nodes cannot be assessed.
Metastasis in one or two regional lymph nodes.
Metastasis in three or more regional lymph nodes.

stage_groups.survival: Not explicitly stated; excellent prognosis with local treatment.
Localized disease has 5-year relative survival of approximately 85% [Discussion MS-10].
Prognosis worse than stage I; survival data not explicitly given.
Prognosis worse than stage IIA; survival data not explicitly given.
Stage III disease has significantly worse outcomes compared to earlier stages [Discussion MS-6].
Poor prognosis; survival data not explicitly given.
5-year relative survival of only approximately 42% for stage IV disease [Discussion MS-10].
